purpose.description: Your code implements the core Redux tasks: defines initialWagonState, a reducer with cases for gather, travel, and tippedWagon, and runs a sequence of actions to simulate the game. It initializes and logs the wagon state as required.
The reducer follows the task instructions for basic state transitions and immutability by returning new objects with spread syntax.

readability.description: Nice use of a switch statement and descriptive action types. The code is easy to follow and mirrors the task requirements.
Variables and function names are clear. Consider adding a brief comment above each case to remind what each action represents.

performance.description: The travel case correctly guards against negative supplies by returning current state when funds are insufficient.
Code duplicates state spreading in multiple cases which is fine for this size; if expanding, consider a helper to ensure immutability consistently or to abstract common state updates.
